docs.title: Billing terms and invoice rules
docs.content: Octane invoices weekly on a Thursday-to-Wednesday billing cycle for LOC accounts. Invoices are due within 7 days. Late invoices accrue a 1.5% weekly late fee after 10 days overdue. Partially paid invoices keep their open balance until CMP reconciliation clears them. Prepay accounts are not invoiced; their balance is drawn down per transaction.

docs.title: Line of credit management
docs.content: A carrier's credit limit is set at underwriting and reviewed quarterly. Credit used is calculated from funded transaction totals within the current billing week starting Monday 00:00 ET. When credit remaining reaches zero, cards decline until a payment posts or the limit is raised by Finance.

docs.title: Collections escalation process
docs.content: Debtors are classified by aging buckets: 1-15, 16-30, 31-60, 61-90 and 90+ days overdue. Hard debtors are accounts with any invoice older than 60 days or total debt above $5,000. Collections calls begin at 5 days overdue, LOC suspension happens at 15 days, and accounts move to external collections at 90 days.

docs.title: Application verification checklist
docs.content: Every application requires: FMCSA authority check, EIN match, bank verification through Plaid, credit review via CreditSafe or iSoftPull, and identity verification of the owner. Active clients are re-verified monthly. A failed re-verification freezes new card issuance until compliance clears it.

docs.title: Sales pipeline stages
docs.content: Leads move through First Call, Second Call and Third Call cadences before qualification. Qualified leads submit a WEX application; approval auto-converts the lead to a Deal. Deals progress through Application Review, Approval, Card Provisioning, Billing Setup and finally First Swipe which marks an active client.

docs.title: Retention and win-back playbook
docs.content: Dormant clients are those with no card swipes for 14 days. Retention outreach starts with a check-in call, then a fuel-discount win-back offer at 21 days, and a final executive call at 30 days. Churned clients may be reactivated within 90 days without a new application.

docs.title: Support ticket escalation
docs.content: Support tickets are answered within 4 business hours. Card-decline emergencies are P1 and handled immediately by phone. Billing disputes route to the Billing team; fraud holds route to Verification. Any ticket unresolved for 48 hours escalates to the CS manager.

docs.title: Finance reporting cadence
docs.content: Finance produces a weekly revenue report every Monday covering funded volume, discount spread, and fee income. Month-end close reconciles CMP invoices against Zoho payment modules (MX Merchant, Zelle, Chase, Stripe). Cash-flow forecasts are updated bi-weekly.

docs.title: Fuel card basics
docs.content: Octane fuel cards work at major truck stop networks nationwide. Drivers authorize at the pump with a card number plus driver ID and unit number prompts. Discounts are applied at point of sale and shown on the weekly invoice. Money codes provide one-time funds for emergencies.

docs.title: About Octane
docs.content: Octane is a US fuel card company serving owner-operators and small fleets. The company partners with WEX for card issuing and EFS for card management, with customer data managed in Zoho CRM and support in Zoho Desk.
